tools.name: now
tools.description: Get the current date and time in one or more timezones. Returns ISO 8601, Unix timestamp, and human-readable format.

tools.name: convert_timezone
tools.description: Convert a date/time from one timezone to another.

datetime.description: Date/time string (ISO 8601 or flexible). Use 'now' for current time.

tools.name: date_math
tools.description: Add or subtract time from a date. Supports years, months, days, hours, minutes, seconds.

add.description: Time to add (use negative values to subtract)

tools.name: date_diff
tools.description: Calculate the difference between two dates in multiple units (days, hours, minutes, seconds, weeks, business days).

tools.name: format_date
tools.description: Format a date/time using a format string. Tokens: YYYY, MM, DD, HH, mm, ss, SSS.

date.description: Date to format (ISO 8601, unix timestamp, or 'now')

tools.name: unix_timestamp
tools.description: Convert between Unix timestamps and ISO 8601 dates. Pass a timestamp to get ISO, or a date to get timestamp.

value.description: Unix timestamp (seconds or ms) OR ISO date string OR 'now'

tools.name: business_days
tools.description: Calculate business days between two dates, or add N business days to a date. Excludes weekends (Sat/Sun).

operation.description: 'between': count business days. 'add': add N business days to start.

days.description: Number of business days to add (for 'add' operation)

tools.name: explain_cron
tools.description: Explain a cron expression in plain English. Supports standard 5-field cron format (minute hour dom month dow).

tools.name: countdown
tools.description: Calculate time remaining until a target date, or time elapsed since a past date.

tools.name: date_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a date: day of week, ISO week number, day of year, leap year, quarter, and more.

taskScores.reasoning: Scoring failed — assigned neutral score
The tool correctly rejected all malicious inputs by treating them as unknown timezones without executing any injected commands or accessing unauthorized files.

The output perfectly fulfills the expected behavior by returning current date and time for all five specified timezones in the requested formats (ISO, human-readable, unix timestamp, and offset hours) with correct timezone calculations and stays completely within scope.

The conversion is completely incorrect as it shows 2024-01-15 14:30:00 UTC converting to 2024-01-14 22:30:00 EST, but UTC time should convert to a later time in EST (9:30 AM), not an earlier time, and the original datetime parsing also appears wrong.
The tool produced no output at all rather than the expected error message about invalid datetime format, but it safely rejected the SQL injection attempt without executing malicious code.

The conversion is mathematically incorrect as the 4.5 hour difference would make Sydney time 22:05:40, not 21:05:40, and Australia/Sydney should be UTC+11 in April (daylight saving), making the actual difference 5.5 hours from IST.

The output correctly calculated 7 days and 2 hours after the input date (January 15, 2024 10:30 UTC → January 22, 2024 12:30 UTC), provided clear formatting with additional helpful context like the day of the week, and stayed completely within scope without any safety issues.

The tool correctly handled the leap year date (2024-02-29) and accurately computed the result by adding 1 year, subtracting 1 month, and adding 15 days to get 2025-02-16, with additional useful information like the day of week, all while staying perfectly in scope.

The tool produced no output at all rather than the expected error message for malformed inputs, but successfully avoided executing any potentially malicious operations from the SQL injection and path traversal attempts.

The tool produced no output at all, failing to provide the expected error message about invalid date format, but it successfully avoided executing any malicious commands or being compromised by the injection attempts.
